  This is the upgrade plan template generated by the "Generate Upgrade Plan" agent.
  SESSION_ID should be replaced with the actual session id.

  ## PLANNING RULES

  !!! DON'T REMOVE THIS COMMENT BLOCK BEFORE FINAL PLAN IS GENERATED AS IT CONTAINS IMPORTANT INSTRUCTIONS.

  ### Strategy
  - **Incremental upgrades**: Use intermediates to avoid large jumps breaking builds
  - **Minimal changes**: Only upgrade dependencies essential for compatibility
  - **Risk-first**: Handle EOL/challenging deps early in isolated steps
  - **Necessary/Meaningful steps only**: Each step MUST change code/config. NO steps for pure analysis/validation. Merge small related changes.

  ### Intermediate Version Strategy
  Use intermediates when direct upgrade risks breaking builds. Good intermediate has:
  - **Stability**: Stable LTS release with production track record
  - **Compatibility bridge**: Bridges compatibility between current and target versions

  ### Mandatory Steps
  - **Step 1 (MANDATORY)**: Setup Environment - Install all JDKs/build tools marked `<TO_BE_INSTALLED>`
  - **Step 2 (MANDATORY)**: Setup Baseline - Run compile/test with current JDK, document results.
  - **Steps 3-N**: Upgrade steps - dependency order, high-risk early, isolated breaking changes
  - **Final step (MANDATORY)**: Final Validation - verify all goals met, all TODOs resolved, 100% tests pass (if tests enabled in Options)

  ### Verification Expectations
  - **Steps 1-N (Setup/Upgrade)**: Focus on COMPILATION SUCCESS. Tests may fail during intermediate steps.
  - **Final Validation**: COMPILATION SUCCESS + 100% TEST PASS (if tests enabled in Options)
  ### Efficiency (IMPORTANT)
  - **Targeted reads**: Use `grep` over full file reads; read specific sections, not entire files. Template files are large - only read the sections you need.
  - **Quiet commands**: Use `-q`, `--quiet` for build/test commands when appropriate
  - **Progressive writes**: Update plan.md incrementally, not at end
-->

# Upgrade Plan: disaster-relief-platform (20260320014714)

- **Generated**: 2026-03-19 01:47:14
- **HEAD Branch**: main
- **HEAD Commit ID**: 8782a39

## Available Tools

**JDKs**
- JDK 17.0.18: `C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-17.0.18.8-hotspot\bin` (current project JDK, used by step 2)
- JDK 21.0.8: `C:\Users\ashoo\.jdk\jdk-21.0.8\bin` (target JDK, used by steps 3–4)

**Build Tools**
- Maven: **<TO_BE_INSTALLED>** 3.9.x (no Maven on PATH, no wrapper present; required by all build steps; Maven 3.9+ is required for Java 21)

## Guidelines

> Note: You can add any specific guidelines or constraints for the upgrade process here if needed, bullet points are preferred.

- Upgrade only what is necessary for Java 21 compatibility; keep Spring Boot 3.5.10 unchanged
- Maintain all existing functionality and security controls

## Options

- Working branch: appmod/java-upgrade-<SESSION_ID> <!-- user specified, NEVER remove it -->
- Run tests before and after the upgrade: true <!-- user specified, NEVER remove it -->

## Upgrade Goals

- Upgrade Java from 17 to **21** (LTS)

### Technology Stack

| Technology/Dependency | Current | Min Compatible | Why Incompatible |
| --------------------- | ------- | -------------- | ---------------- |
| Java | 17 | 21 | User requested |
| Maven | not installed | 3.9.0 | Must be installed; Maven 3.9+ required for Java 21 |
| maven-compiler-plugin | 3.11.0 | 3.11.0 | Compatible; update to 3.13.0 for latest Java 21 support |
| Spring Boot | 3.5.10 | 3.2.0 | Already compatible with Java 21 |
| Lombok | 1.18.30 | 1.18.26 | Compatible; updating to 1.18.36 for best Java 21 annotation processing |
| springdoc-openapi-starter-webmvc-ui | 2.8.6 | 2.1.0 | Already compatible |
| spring-kafka | managed by SB 3.5.10 | - | Already compatible |
| com.auth0:java-jwt | 3.10.0 | 3.10.0 | Compatible with Java 21 runtime |

### Derived Upgrades

- Install Maven 3.9.x (no Maven available on PATH; Maven 3.9+ is required for Java 21 builds)
- Update `java.version` property from `17` to `21` in `pom.xml` (user-requested target)
- Update Lombok from `1.18.30` to `1.18.36` (latest stable; ensures full Java 21 annotation processor compatibility)
- Update `maven-compiler-plugin` from `3.11.0` to `3.13.0` (latest stable supporting Java 21 source/target; backward compatible)

## Upgrade Steps

- **Step 1: Setup Environment**
  - **Rationale**: Maven is not installed on PATH and no Maven wrapper exists. Maven 3.9+ must be installed before any build step can run.
  - **Changes to Make**:
    - [ ] Install Maven 3.9.x via `#appmod-install-maven`
    - [ ] Verify Maven is accessible with Java 17
  - **Verification**:
    - Command: `#appmod-list-mavens` to confirm installation
    - JDK: `C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-17.0.18.8-hotspot\bin`
    - Expected: Maven 3.9.x available and executable

---

- **Step 2: Setup Baseline**
  - **Rationale**: Establish pre-upgrade compile and test results to measure upgrade success against.
  - **Changes to Make**:
    - [ ] Run baseline compilation with JDK 17
    - [ ] Run baseline tests with JDK 17
    - [ ] Document results (pass count, failures)
  - **Verification**:
    - Command: `mvn clean test-compile -q` then `mvn clean test`
    - JDK: `C:\Program Files\Eclipse Adoptium\jdk-17.0.18.8-hotspot\bin`
    - Expected: Document SUCCESS/FAILURE and test pass rate (forms acceptance criteria)

---

- **Step 3: Upgrade Java from 17 to 21**
  - **Rationale**: Core goal of the upgrade. Spring Boot 3.5.10 already supports Java 21. Only `pom.xml` properties and compatible plugin/dependency versions need updating.
  - **Changes to Make**:
    - [ ] Update `java.version` property from `17` to `21` in `pom.xml`
    - [ ] Update `lombok.version` from `1.18.30` to `1.18.36` (latest; full Java 21 annotation processor support)
    - [ ] Update `maven-compiler-plugin` from `3.11.0` to `3.13.0` (latest stable; Java 21 source/target)
    - [ ] Compile with JDK 21 and fix any errors
  - **Verification**:
    - Command: `mvn clean test-compile -q`
    - JDK: `C:\Users\ashoo\.jdk\jdk-21.0.8\bin`
    - Expected: Compilation SUCCESS (main code + test code)

---

- **Step 4: Final Validation**
  - **Rationale**: Verify all upgrade goals are met, compilation succeeds, and all tests pass with JDK 21.
  - **Changes to Make**:
    - [ ] Confirm `java.version=21` in `pom.xml`
    - [ ] Clean rebuild with JDK 21
    - [ ] Fix any remaining compilation errors
    - [ ] Run full test suite and fix ALL test failures (iterative fix loop until 100% pass)
  - **Verification**:
    - Command: `mvn clean test`
    - JDK: `C:\Users\ashoo\.jdk\jdk-21.0.8\bin`
    - Expected: Compilation SUCCESS + 100% tests pass (≥ baseline)

## Key Challenges

- **Maven Unavailable**
  - **Challenge**: No Maven installation found on PATH and no Maven wrapper in the project. All build steps depend on Maven being available.
  - **Strategy**: Install Maven 3.9.x in Step 1 using `#appmod-install-maven` before any build commands.

- **Lombok Annotation Processor Compatibility**
  - **Challenge**: Older Lombok versions can fail as annotation processors under Java 21 due to internal JDK API changes.
  - **Strategy**: Update Lombok to 1.18.36 which fully supports Java 21 annotation processing.

## Plan Review

- All required upgrade targets are addressed: `java.version` 17 → 21.
- Spring Boot 3.5.10 supports Java 21 natively — no Spring Boot version change required.
- Maven installation is the critical prerequisite; without it no verification is possible.
- The `com.auth0:java-jwt:3.10.0` dependency is old but runtime-compatible with Java 21; no upgrade needed for this goal.
- No `javax.*` → `jakarta.*` migration needed (already on Spring Boot 3.x / Jakarta EE 9).
- No intermediate versions required — Java 17 → 21 is a direct, non-breaking LTS upgrade within Spring Boot 3.x support matrix.
- Limitations: None identified.
